JUDICIAL MEDIATION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Defendant Leave To Complete Discovery Proceedings; Declaration Of Tara Macomber
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TIMOTHY ANTHONY BONNICI'S Motion For Order Granting Defendant Leave To Complete Discovery Proceedings.

Transferred to be heard on the court's discovery calendar in room #301 on the same date at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To Complete Discovery Proceedings (Incorrectly Set For 9:30 A.M. Dept. 302 Law & Motion)
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tara Macomber, Esq. of OPEN DOOR LEGAL, for defendant/moving party;
No other appearance.

Defendant's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: There being no objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on TIMOTHY BONNICI'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To Complete Discovery Proceedings as follows:

Defendant's Motion For Order Granting Defendant Leave To Complete Discovery Proceedings "Motion" is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant's reply substantially narrows the relief requested in the Motion. Defendant seeks only to re-open discovery so he "can use the testimony of Plaintiff's deposition and the evidence [of] the subpoenaed records from the Daly City police department in the consolidated trial" (Reply at p. 5), referring to CGC-17-557688. No party cites authority stating that evidence obtained in discovery in only one of two cases consolidated for trial cannot be used at the consolidated trial. Nonetheless, the Motion is granted in part and discovery is re-opened solely for the purpose of allowing either party to use at the consolidated trial-as long as it is permitted under the Evidence Code and by the judge trying the cases-Plaintiff's deposition testimony from CGC-17-557688 and evidence produced pursuant to Defendant's subpoena to the Daly City Police Department.

Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-31-2019 Continued To Apr-20-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-557688 WITH CASE CGC-16-553340
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Tara maComber, Esq. of OPEN DOOR LEGAL, T:415.735.4124, for defendant/moving party;
Richard Liebermann, Esq. of the LAW OFFICES OF MICHAEL LIEBERMANN, T:415.929.3197, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT TIMOTHY ANTHONY BONNICI'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-557688 WITH CASE CGC-16-553340, as follows:

Defendant Timothy Bonnici's motion to consolidate cases is granted. The two related cases involve common questions of law or fact, and consolidation would further judicial economy. (Code Civ. Proc. 1048.) The cases are consolidated for purposes of trial only. The parties should be able to complete any remaining discovery in Case No. CGC-17-557688, which was filed in March 2017, before the current April 20, 2020 trial date.

Counsel for defendant to submit by email a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.
